gzipMethod: zlib.gzipSync (default level 6); both sides identical → fair comparison
scopeNote: JS file size only; performance proxy, not a runtime benchmark.

source.bundleMethod: bun build with externals [elkjs, entities] matching tsup.config.ts

commands.output: beautiful-mermaid raw: 328094 gzip: 66816; ours raw: 127082 gzip: 41785

commands.purpose: independent raw byte count — our entry chunk
independent gzip byte count (zlib default level 6) — our entry chunk

commands.cmd: bun build references/beautiful-mermaid/src/index.ts --external elkjs --external entities --outfile $TMPDIR/bm-bundle.js (via execSync inside bun -e, matching sh/size-report.js)

findings.detail: Report uses zlib.gzipSync default (level 6), not level 9 as docs/size/size-plan.md §4.1 recommends. Both sides use the identical method so the comparison is internally fair. Level-9 reference values recorded in docs/size/verification.md for transparency.

ours.rawBytes measures only the entry chunk (index-HASH.js), not the aggregate of all Vite-render-path chunks. docs/size/size-plan.md §3.2 recommends aggregating reachable chunks. This is a known methodology gap deferred to a future size-loop iteration; it does not affect the current verification that data is real (not estimated).
